suggestion: This topic is fundamentally unsuitable for a top ML conference. It (1) contains no machine learning methodology, (2) builds on discarded physics theories (Kelvin's vortex atoms were abandoned over a century ago), (3) explicitly instructs ignoring contradictory evidence which is anti-scientific, and (4) incorporates esoteric/hermetic principles rather than testable hypotheses. REFUNDAMENTALLY REFRAME: 'Graph neural networks for predicting magnetic properties from crystallographic symmetry group descriptors' — use established databases (Materials Project, AFLOW), standard crystal symmetry features, and modern GNN architectures. This keeps the legitimate interest in structure-property relationships while using actual ML on sound scientific foundations.
